This short-lived tornado snapped the trunks of a few large trees southwest of Christie, WI. The diameter of the largest tree that was snapped was large enough to support a preliminary EF1 rating.
This tornado developed in rural areas of Clark County, WI between Christie and Loyal. As the tornado moved northeast, it damaged multiple trees and removed a portion of the roof of a farm building.
Law enforcement reported a tornado on the ground near Loyal, WI.

This tornado developed in fields west of Unity before tracking northeast toward Colby. As it moved through rural areas of northeastern Clark County, it damaged multiple farm outbuildings, trees, power poles, and a home. This home had the majority of its roof removed, forming the basis of the preliminary EF2 rating. The tornado tracked through the southeastern edge of Colby damaging another home, multiple outbuildings, power poles, and a car dealership before dissipating.
Hail ranged from quarter to half dollar size.
Hail fell from 6:39 to 6:47. Majority of hail was small but largest stone was measured at 1 inch in diameter.
Majority of stones were penny to quarter size.

EF-1. Tornado was on the ground for about 6.5 miles and 13 minutes through mostly rural areas damaging trees along its path and one outbuilding roof. The tornado was at EF1 intensity with maximum winds to about 100 mph in Houston county. The tornado did cause a tornado debris signature on radar.
TDS on radar.

EF-1. This brief tornado ripped the roof off a mobile home residence, damaged nearby trees, knocked farming implements on their side, and heavily damaged a barn on 363rd Ave. It weakened as it continued northeast across a field for about 1 mile, causing minor tree damage near the intersection of County Road 17 and 355th Ave where it dissipated. Max wind 100 mph.
Utility pole down on main street near the post office. Crews on scene.
Spotter reported tornado on the ground looking north from CR 17 and Hwy 65.
EF-0. This brief tornado snapped some limbs off trees near Interstate 90 at Nodine Hill. Max wind 70 mph.
EF-0. This very brief tornado touched down at the edge of a wooded area off County Road 20 and damaged a few pine trees in a narrow path. Max wind of 75 mph.
911 Center reported tornado on the ground south of Hokah.
Video relayed via social media of a tornado on the ground south of Lake City.

This tornado developed near State Highway 33 several miles south of Barre Mills, causing extensive tree damage to the Valleywood Springs area before moving northeast, causing additional spotty tree damage before dissipating near the intersection of County Highways M and I. Valleywood Springs would be affected by a second tornado around 10 minutes later. Each tornado had a distinct circulation and debris signature on radar.
TDS on radar with visual confirmation from office before becoming clouded in rain.
TDS noted on radar.

This short lived tornado developed east of La Crosse and affected the Valleywood Springs neighborhood causing damage to trees as it moved east. Valleywood Springs had already been affected by a tornado around 10 minutes earlier. Each tornado had a distinct circulation and debris signature on radar.

EF-0. This tornado developed very close to the Iowa-Minnesota state line, causing damage to trees as it moved east northeast. Despite causing damage to trees within proximity of structures, no structural damage was observed. Max wind of 75 mph.
TDS on radar and report of tornado on the ground from dispatch.

Drone images, spotter funnel video, and EM pictures confirmed an EF0 tornado path of 1/2 mile long beginning northwest of town. Maximum winds were 75 mph and the path width was approximated at 40 yards. Minor damage to corn, an outbuilding, silo, and roof shingles occurred. The tornado was on the ground for about 3 minutes. Time estimated from radar.
